item.title: 【used】Ernie Ball Music Man / StingRay 1989 mod. 1989 4.265kg #29693【GIB Yokohama】 — $2,207
item.description:
$2,207 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Musicman emerged as Leo Fender's second brand and became beloved by bassists worldwide.
Many artists, including Louis Johnson and Flea, are known to use their basses. (Titles omitted)

The Stingray is a classic model that enjoys overwhelming popularity within the brand.
Alongside Fender's Jazz Bass and Precision Bass, it's safe to say it's one of the most popular basses in the world.

This particular instrument is from 1989.
It has several modifications.

The body is made of ash.
The neck and fretboard are maple.

The body has been refinished with a slightly rough-textured paint.
There is also paint chipping on the body top, likely from slap bass playing.

This suggests that the instrument has been used for many years.

The pickups have been replaced with Saymour Duncan SMB-4A pickups.
They use Alnico magnets and produce a warm sound.

The preamp has been replaced with a Saymour Duncan STC-3M4 Music Man Active Preamp.

The E-string tuning machine has been replaced with a HIPSHOT D-Tuner.

It boasts the powerful, punchy low end and crisp attack characteristic of a StingRay.

With its 3-band EQ, it offers a wide range of sound shaping possibilities, producing a distinctive sound in various genres, including rock and funk.

The frets have approximately 70% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have been refinished. The paint texture is rough.

There is a paint chip of about 8cm on the body top, likely from slapping.

There is a hole near the headstock logo, possibly from a string guide.

Overall, there are signs of use, and the metal parts show some tarnishing.

The electronics are in good condition.

The E string tuning peg has been replaced with a HIPSHOT D-tuner.

The pickup has been replaced with a Saymour Duncan SMB-4A.

The preamp has been replaced with a Saymour Duncan STC-3M4 Music Man Active Preamp.

The bass pot and jack have been replaced at GIB.

Controls: Master Volume, Treble, Middle, Bass
Nut Width: 42.7mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 1989
Country of Manufacture: USA

This guitar will be delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by a GIB craftsman.

Accessories: Gig bag

Text: Tomoki Hayakawa

Inspection 담당: Ryuzaki

Product Photos: Higashie

GIB 3-Month Warranty Included

◯GIB and TONIQ Warranty Information

●Lifetime Free Maintenance

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free "String Vibration Setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

・Please present your warranty card with your name filled in.

・Only applicable to the person who purchased the instrument at a GIB or TONIQ physical store or online shop.

・Please bear the cost of shipping and other miscellaneous expenses.

●Re-purchase Price Guarantee

If you sell your purchased item within 6 months of purchase, we guarantee the buyback price. The price is 50% for new items and 70% for used items.

●Defect Warranty

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. With the exception of some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.

The warranty period is 3 months for used products and 1 year for new products.

Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:

* Malfunctions and damage caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.

* Replacement of consumables.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual.

* Malfunctions or damage to parts repaired or modified by a party other than our company.

* If the warranty certificate does not have your name, model name, serial number, etc. filled in, or if the wording has been altered.

* If the warranty certificate is not presented or is lost.

(Warranty is only provided for purchases made in Japan.)

● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in

When you use our trade-in service at the time of purchase, the sales price will be 10% off. (Consignment items are excluded. This offer is only applicable if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the selling price.)

Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.

We will ship with sufficient packaging.

We also ship internationally.

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: Gibson EB-2 1969 - Walnut — $5,024.19
item.description:
$5,024.19 · Very Good · Daughterson's Vintage Guitars

Gibson EB-2w – All Original, Great Condition from 1969

Up for sale is a Gibson EB-2w in good condition – a true vintage classic with tons of character and thunderous tone.

  • All original and fully functional
  • Currently strung with La Bella 760FL flats for that deep, woody thump
  • Includes a non-original but perfectly fitting Epiphone hardshell case for safe transport and storage

A previous owner installed a thumbrest that has since been removed – you can still see the small screw holes, but they don’t affect playability or structural integrity.

This bass absolutely roars. The low end is massive – thick, warm, and round, easily the biggest you’ll ever hear. Flip the tone switch, and the sound instantly tightens up, cutting through with a more modern, articulate voice.

A stunning piece of Gibson bass history, equally at home in the studio, on stage, or in a serious collection. If you’re after that legendary EB-2 punch and vibe, this one delivers.

item.title: Kingston Vintage Japanese Bass 1970's Sunburst — $600
item.description:
$600 · Good · Alex's Gear

Selling a 70s Kingston bass made in Japan.

This bass is very unique!

Has some Ernie Ball flatwound strings on it, make an offer!

Free pickup in Nashville, also willing to ship elsewhere!

item.title: BASSO ELETTRICO EKO JB-300 Black — $190.72
item.description:
$190.72 · Brand New · Firefly Audio

BASSO ELETTRICO EKO JB-300 Black


BASSO ELETTRICO NERO
Il JB-300 Sunburst è il basso elettrico 4 corde della serie "Tribute 300" di casa Eko Guitars. Ispirato al leggendario Jazz Bass e caratterizzato da hardware vintage, presenta corpo in Pioppo, manico in Acero e tastiera 21 tasti in resina Eko WPC. Monta due pickup JB con controlli separati di volume e 1 tono. Ideale per il bassista che desidera uno strumento affidabile, versatile, performante e ad un costo contenuto.

  • Finitura: Gloss

  • Finitura: Gloss

  • Capotasto: 42mm

item.title: 【new】Cort / GB-Fusion 5 Antique Brown Burst 4.38kg #IE250701100【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,455
item.description:
$1,455 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Cort is a leading guitar brand from South Korea.

They own their own factory and have a global presence, particularly in OEM manufacturing. For example, Ibanez and PRS guitars are famously manufactured at Cort's factory.

This in-house brand utilizes the knowledge gained from OEM manufacturing.

The GB Fusion 5 is based on the classic JB style.

By incorporating modern specifications throughout, it successfully embodies timeless value in a way that suits the modern era.

The body features an alder back and a spalted maple top.

It is characterized by its classic sound and exotic look.

The neck is made of hard maple, and the fingerboard is made of birdseye maple, a classic construction.

Birdseye maple is said to only appear in very hard maple.

The number of frets is 22. This is the same as the Sadowsky Will Lee model, for example.

Personally, I think this is the maximum number of frets that can be used while maintaining a classic sound.

The fretboard radius is designed at 12 inches.

The fret edges are very smooth, so you won't feel any stress on them.
The side position markers use Luminlay, demonstrating the attention to detail in its construction.

The original pickups, called Voiced Tone, were designed by Cort's engineers.
They normally work with top brands like Nordstrand, Bartolini, and PRS, and the quality of the pickups they designed, based on their accumulated knowledge, is comparable to those brands.

The preamp features an original 3-band EQ type.

It powerfully and flexibly shapes and outputs the signal created by the pickups, which are designed to emulate vintage tones.

The tuning machines are the globally renowned Hipshot. Ultralite.

It seems to prevent neck dive while simultaneously adding a light and rich midrange to the sound.

When you actually hold it, it naturally gives a JB-like impression. The balance is excellent and it doesn't strain your body, making it a superb design that allows you to concentrate on playing.

The sound, crafted with high-quality electronics, is very impressive, truly a modern JB.

A masterpiece with excellent hardware and tonewoods carefully incorporated.

As expected from an Indonesian-made instrument, it seems to have a price advantage over other brands.

This is a must-try for anyone who has a negative impression of low-priced or Indonesian-made basses. You'll be surprised.

We deliver the highest possible quality through re-inspection and correction by GIB.

●SPECS

Body Back: Alder
Top Wood: Spalted Maple
Neck Wood: Hard Maple
Fingerboard: Birdseye Maple
Construction: Bolt-On
Scale Length: 34″ (864mm)
Neck Shape: C Shape
Frets: 22 / Medium-Jumbo (Nickel Silver)
Fingerboard Radius: 12″ (305mm)
Nut Width: 1 7/8″ (47.6mm)
Inlay: Black Dots with Luminous Side Dots
Tuners: Hipshot® Ultralite
Bridge: MetalCraft M5 (String Spacing: 18mm)
Pickups: Voiced Tone VTB-ST 5-String Pickup Set
Electronics: Active 3-band EQ
Controls: 1-Volume, 1-Blend, 3-Band EQ
Hardware Color: Black
Finish: Gloss (Body) / Matte (Neck)
Color: Antique Brown Burst (ABB)
Strings: D'Addario® EXL170-5SL
Accessories: Gig bag, wrench
Text: Shunsuke Saga
Product photos: Muragishi

Manufacturer's warranty included

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan)

item.title: 【new】Cort / GB64JJ Black 4.5kg #IE251205028【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $920
item.description:
$920 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Cort is a leading guitar brand from South Korea.

They own their own factory and have a global presence, particularly in OEM manufacturing.

For example, Ibanez and PRS guitars are famously manufactured at Cort's factory.

This in-house brand is manufactured utilizing the knowledge gained from OEM production.

This is the GB64JJ model, based on the classic JB style, but incorporating practical specifications demanded by modern musicians in wood selection and electronics.

The body is made of soft maple.

With an ergonomically designed offset body, it boasts comfortable playability whether standing or sitting.

The neck is made of roasted maple, and the fingerboard is made of roasted hard maple.

Heat treatment enhances the rigidity and resistance to environmental changes of the wood, making it not only resistant to warping and twisting, but also adding a rich resonance like a vintage instrument that has been played for many years.

The black block inlays on the fingerboard also function as a cool accent to the look.

This bass adheres to a traditional design with a 34-inch scale length, 38mm nut width, and 20 frets.

The fingerboard radius is set at 9.45 inches (R240), providing a natural and comfortable playability that allows for smooth fingering while preserving the classic JB feel.

Furthermore, the neck joint utilizes a plate to maintain the traditional bass resonance and feel, while the ergonomic neck joint improves access to the higher positions, incorporating modern design elements for comfortable playability.

The pickups are Voiced Tone VTB-STs, uniquely designed by Cort's engineers.

The company's accumulated know-how, gained from handling numerous top-brand pickups, is utilized to produce a musical and expressive sound that mimics vintage tones.

In addition, the control section features 1 Volume, 1 Blend, and an active 2-band EQ. The volume knob allows for active and passive switching via push/pull, offering flexible sound shaping to suit any situation, from the organic, warm tone of passive mode to powerful, modern tones utilizing the built-in EQ.

The hardware, including the bridge and open-gear tuning machines, employs a vintage style, maintaining consistency with the instrument's overall classic concept.

While possessing the timeless appeal of a JB, it incorporates modern features such as roasted maple and high-quality active circuitry, truly embodying a modern classic.

As expected from an Indonesian-made instrument, it boasts a significant price advantage over other brands.

This is a must-try for anyone who has a negative impression of the quality of lower-priced or Asian-made basses.

You'll undoubtedly be surprised.

We deliver the highest possible quality through a re-inspection and setup by Geek IN Box.

Specifications

Construction: Bolt-On
Body Material: Soft Maple
Scale Length: 34″ (864mm)
Neck Material: Roasted Maple
Fingerboard Material: Roasted Hard Maple
Fingerboard Radius: 9.45″ (R240)
Number of Frets: 20 Frets
Inlay: Black Block
Nut Width: 1 1/2″ (38mm)
Pickups: Cort® Voiced Tone VTB-ST
Electronics: 1-Volume, 1-Blend, 2-Band EQ with Active-Passive Push-pull
Bridge: Vintage
Tuning Machines: Open Gear Tuners
Hardware: Chrome
Strings: D'Addario® EXL165
Colors: Natural (NAT), Black (BK)

This guitar will be delivered after being adjusted by GIB's craftsmen for easy playability.

Accessories: Gig bag, wrench

item.title: ATELIER Z M#245 (NAT/M) [Atelier Z × IKEBE Original Pouch Gift Included] — $2,686.36
item.description:
$2,686.36 · Brand New · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

We are receiving the customer order with first comes first serves basis both on website and at real shop.
So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

[Atelier Z × IKEBE Original Pouch Gift]
We're giving away an original pouch (not for sale) created through a collaboration between Atelier Z and IKEBE!
It fits in the pocket of your gig bag and is convenient for storing maintenance tools like wrenches that tend to get scattered around!
This is a rare item that can only be obtained by those who purchase an Atelier Z bass guitar at Ikebe. Take this opportunity to find your dream Atelier Z!

Eligible products: Purchase an ATELIER Z bass guitar priced at 300,000 yen or more (including tax) and receive a free gift. *This is a limited-quantity gift, so please note that it will be discontinued once supplies run out.
*Some items, such as outlet items, sale items, reused/second-hand items, and made-to-order items, may be excluded.
----------------------------------------
The M (Magunitude) #245 was released as a signature model for Tomohito Aoki, but it is now used by countless musicians, both professional and amateur, and is a standard model and a representative model of Atelier Z.

Based on the JB tone of the 70s, it boasts clear highs and a powerful low end, and the sound produced by the densely grained ash body delivers the quintessential scooped bass sound. The control system consists only of two volume controls and a 2-band EQ boost for treble and bass, but opening the back panel reveals a Mid-CUT trim, allowing you to adjust it to your preferred settings.
Attention to detail is evident in features such as the neck angle, making it easy to produce sound even with a light touch. It can handle a variety of playing styles, from the bold sound of his signature slap technique to the thick tone of fingerstyle playing and the attack of pick playing, making it a versatile instrument that can be used in any genre.
Furthermore, this model incorporates the Spectrum Boost that Mr. Aoki had been seeking, resulting in a significantly increased sense of power compared to previous models of the same type. In addition to the bold sound he is known for when slapping, it offers a new sonic potential that can handle a wide range of genres.

Body: Ash 2 Pieces
Neck: Maple 1 Piece 34inch
Fingerboard: Maple, 20 Frets
Pickups : Atelier z Magnitude JBZ-4
Bridge: Atelier z BB419
Preamp: Bartorini XTCT
Controls: M.Vol, Balancer, M.Tone/sw, Treble/Bass
Weight: ≈4.03kg

Gig bag included

item.title: Epiphone 2024 Rex Brown Thunderbird — $920.23
item.description:
$920.23 · Very Good · TC GAKKI

Welcome to TC Gakki! All photos are of the item for sale. Please note they are for sale in our store and may sell out. Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask any questions!

Product Details
________________________________

A signature model of PANTERA bassist Rex Brown!

Rex Brown, the bassist of PANTERA, the legendary band that revolutionized the 90s metal scene, is known as one of the "masters of heavy bass" in the heavy rock world, with his undulating basslines that draw on his jazz background and his tight, overwhelming groove.

This instrument is the Rex Brown Thunderbird, a signature model that embodies his dedication to his craft.

It features a traditional neck-through construction, a black finish, and gold hardware for a luxurious look.

The Epiphone Rex Brown Thunderbird uses a passive circuit and has 2 volume/1 tone controls.

Rex Brown's signature is on the back of the headstock, and his illustration is on the back panel.

It has a powerful and tight output, but is characterized by a raw, sticky midrange.

It has a hard, attacking tone, and the distorted drive sound is exceptional.

The Rex Brown Thunderbird from Gibson features an active circuit and a set neck construction, while this Epiphone version offers more traditional specifications.
Recommended for Rex Brown fans and anyone looking for a cool-looking Thunderbird!

【SPEC】
BODY: Mahogany
NECK: Okoume or Sapelli/Walnut
FINGERBOARD: Indian Laurel
P.U: Epiphone ProBucker 760
CONTROL: 2Vol/Tone
WEIGHT: 3.98kg
SCALE: 34”
NUT WIDTH: 41mm
w/Hardcase

*Neck and electronics have been maintained by our repair technician and are in good condition.
*String height is set to approximately 1.6mm at the 12th fret.
*Parts have been disassembled and cleaned.

[Repair/Modification History/Replacement Parts/External Condition, etc.]
*This information is based on our inspection of used/vintage items.
Please understand in advance.

*Thorough adjustment by our store, with a reliable 5-year adjustment warranty.
*Please feel free to contact our staff for product details.

In charge: Oda

______________________________
Please note that all items are for sale in our Tokyo store.
There is a chance they might be sold out. All items are shipped tracked and insured. All import duties or customs fees are the buyers responsibility - please be aware of the requirements for your country. If you have any questions please ask.

item.description:
$741 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Cort is a leading guitar brand from South Korea.

They own their own factory and have a global presence, particularly in OEM manufacturing.

For example, Ibanez and PRS guitars are famously manufactured at Cort's factory.

This in-house brand is manufactured utilizing the knowledge gained through OEM manufacturing.

This is the GB34JJ, a modern classic model based on the classic JB style, equipped with an active circuit that allows for a wider range of sound shaping.

The body is made of poplar, which is lightweight and has a neutral, natural acoustic characteristic.

The neck is made of hard maple, and the fingerboard is made of jatoba.

Jatoba is an extremely hard and durable wood, used in flooring and high-end furniture, but its properties also produce a fast, clear attack, making it a valued material for bass fingerboards.

The neck features a 34-inch scale, a 38mm nut width, and a traditional 20-fret specification.

The fretboard radius is designed at 9.45 inches (R240), providing natural playability while preserving a classic feel.

Furthermore, the neck joint utilizes a plate to maintain the traditional bass resonance and feel, while the ergonomically designed Ergonomic Neck Joint improves access to the upper frets, incorporating modern design elements for comfortable playability.

The pickups are Cort's original Powersound JJ Style set.

The neck pickup is meticulously designed to deliver a silky, smooth tone, while the bridge pickup provides a clear, tight, and singing character.

In addition, it features an active 2-band EQ preamp.

While building upon the traditional tone created by the vintage-like pickups, the EQ allows for powerful and flexible sound shaping, making it instantly adaptable to a wide range of genres and ensembles.

The hardware, such as the bridge and open-gear tuning machines, follows a vintage style, maintaining consistency with the instrument's overall classic concept.

A masterpiece that possesses the timeless appeal of a classic JB bass while incorporating modern innovations and the benefits of active electronics throughout.

As is typical of Indonesian-made basses, it boasts a significant price advantage over other brands.

This is a must-try for anyone who has a negative impression of the quality of lower-priced or Asian-made basses.

You'll undoubtedly be surprised.

We deliver the highest possible quality through a thorough re-inspection and setup by Geek IN Box.

Scale Length: 34″ (864mm)
Neck Material: Hard Maple

Fingerboard Radius: 9.45″ (R240)
Number of Frets: 20 Frets
Inlay: Dot

Pickups: Cort® Powersound JJ Style Pickup Set
Electronics: Active 2 Band Electronics

item.title: Gibson Flying V Bass 1981 - Ebony — $5,084
item.description:
$5,084 · Excellent · Wolfy's collection

For Sale: 1981 Gibson Flying V Bass – Black – Serial #83271022

A rare opportunity to own a Kalamazoo-built Gibson Flying V Bass — one of the most distinctive and collectible Gibson basses ever made. Produced only for a short run in the early 1980s, these instruments are highly sought after, and examples in this condition almost never come up.

Specs & Details:

  • Model/Year: 1981 Gibson Flying V Bass
  • Factory: Kalamazoo, Michigan, USA
  • Serial Number: 83271022
  • Finish: Black
  • Refurbishment (Pro work, 2021):
    • Full refret for flawless playability
    • Enlarged side dots for better visibility on stage
    • Repaint of body and neck (non-nitro), original headstock finish preserved
  • Condition: Excellent for its age – restored for both playing and collecting
  • Tone & Feel: Big Gibson punch, long sustain, comfortable lightweight neck
  • Case: Includes the original Gibson hard case

Why This Bass?

  • Rare model – very limited production in the early ’80s
  • Kalamazoo-made – from Gibson’s legendary original plant
  • Pro refurbished – ready to play live, but equally at home in a serious collection
  • Original hard case included – boosts both value and provenance

Serious enquiries only. These don’t come up often, and when they do, they don’t stick around.

item.title: 【new】Cort / GB24JJ Trans Black 4.08kg #IE250905225【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $704
item.description:
$704 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Cort is a leading guitar brand from South Korea.

They own their own factory and have a global presence, particularly in OEM manufacturing. For example, Ibanez and PRS guitars are famously manufactured at Cort's factory.

This in-house brand is manufactured utilizing the knowledge gained through OEM production.

The GB24JJ is a model with timeless appeal, based on the classic JB style.

The body back is made of poplar, and the body top is made of flame maple.

This contributes to both lightness and aesthetic beauty.

The neck is made of hard maple, and the fretboard is made of jatoba.

Jatoba is hard and durable, and is commonly used in flooring and furniture. Due to its properties, it is also highly valued as a guitar fretboard material.

It has 20 frets, a classic specification.

The fingerboard radius is designed at 9.45″. There's a consistent direction to it.

It features original pickups named Cort® Powersound JJ Style.
The neck pickup is designed for silky smoothness, while the bridge pickup is designed for clarity and a singing character.

By using a joint plate, the playability maintains a Fender-like feel, while the shape of the plate has been modified to improve playability in the high-frequency range.

While fundamentally classic in its design, it incorporates modern touches throughout.
A truly modern classic JB model.

item.title: Fender Squier Classic Vibe 70's Jazz Bass 4 String Electric Bass Guitar - Black — $429
item.description:
$429 · Very Good · Rayhill Audio

The Squier Classic Vibe ‘70s Jazz Bass is a nod to the 1970s evolution of the J Bass, combining the luxurious playability that made it famous with the versatility and massive tone of its dual Fender-Designed alnico single-coil pickups.

Player-friendly features include a slim and comfortable “C”-shaped neck profile with an easy-playing 9.5”-radius fingerboard and narrow-tall frets, as well as a vintage-style bridge with slotted barrel saddles. This throwback Squier model also features 1970s inspired headstock markings, nickel-plated hardware and a slick vintage-tinted gloss neck finish for an old-school aesthetic vibe.

Features:

Poplar body with pickguard and gloss finish

Maple neck with C profile and maple fingerboard

Dual Fender-designed Alnico single-coil pickups

Nickel hardware includes vintage-style bridge and tuning machines

Case: Sold Separately

Weight: 9 lb 4 oz



Condition:

This instrument is in excellent, near mint condition. It has a finish flaw repair on the back of the neck that is smooth to the touch. The techs found no other issues and it has little to no play wear. It looks, plays and sounds great! It still has protective cellophane on the hardware. Please see pictures for overall condition of the guitar. The actual item for sale is shown in the photographs. 

More Info:

We are an authorized dealer for brand approved manufacturer refurbished guitars. These guitars are sent directly from the manufacturer to the authorized refurbishing center where they are fully inspected, set up and cleared by a certified technician. They are sent there for many reasons, including: returns, cosmetic flaws, minor repairs, surplus & discontinued or last years models. The guitars serial number may be partially altered and/or replaced and the back of the headstock is lightly stamped "used" by the authorized refurbishing center per manufacturer agreements. 


Shipping:

Guaranteed free, fast and safe UPS shipping to lower 48 states. International UPS expedited and USPS Priority shipping available to most countries. We ship our items the same or next business day. All items are professionally packed for safe transit. 


Returns:

We back every guitar we sell with a 14 day return policy. If you do not like the way it feels, plays, looks or sounds upon arrival, just let us know and you can return it. See our shop policy link for full details.

item.description:
$1,243.65 · Very Good · Cool Old Guitars

Made in Japan in 1993, the JB62-FL was a non-catalog "Jaco" fretless model Jazz Bass from Fender. Alder body with a maple neck and lined rosewood fingerboard, it weighs 4.1kg's. Fender Japan single coil pickups, spiral saddles on the bridge and reverse wound tuners, it appears all original. 
Very good used condition as pictured with some finish wear and tear from basic use. The neck is straight, the truss works as it should and the pots/output jack sound great.

item.description:

Basswood body with a two-tone sunburst finish, 20 fret maple neck (7.25" radius), it weighs just under 4kg's. Seems to be all original as pictured.   Very good used condition as pictured with some finish wear and tear from basic use. item.title: 【used】Ibanez / EHB1265MS 2023 3.62kg #I230402792【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,642
item.description:
$1,642 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Ibanez is a leading Japanese guitar and bass brand, globally renowned alongside Fender and Gibson.

This is the EHB1265MS, an innovative modern bass featuring an ergonomically designed headless design and multi-scale construction.

While incorporating the previously unpopular headless design, the overall harmonious balance demonstrates the company's high level of design expertise.

The body features a chic walnut top and an ash back.

The chambered, compact, and lightweight body fits perfectly against the body whether standing or sitting, minimizing player fatigue during long playing sessions.

The neck is a robust 9-piece construction combining Panga Panga and walnut, with the fingerboard also using Panga Panga.

It also incorporates graphite reinforcement rods, boasting extremely high rigidity against environmental changes.

The multi-scale design, which gradually changes from 35 inches to 33 inches, achieves both the tight tension of the low-B string and the supple playability of the higher strings.

The fact that the string tension is more uniform also makes the neck more resistant to twisting, which is very logical.

The MR5HS bridge, with its independent string structure, allows for precise adjustment of the string spacing to the player's preference, as the saddles can move ±1.5mm left and right.

The pickups are Bartolini BH2s, renowned for their rich bass and punchy midrange.

The preamp is a Vari-mid 3-band EQ that allows for stepless adjustment of the mid frequency (250Hz to 16kHz).

It can also be instantly switched to passive mode with a single switch.

The generous use of high-quality electronics and hardware, which is usually unthinkable in this price range, is a major appeal unique to Ibanez, a globally distributed company.

The sound is characterized by a moderate airiness, a wide range, and a tight, fast attack.

The clear tone with good separation and stable pitch makes it highly compatible with technical solo playing and modern ensembles utilizing multiple strings.

This is an ambitious work that brings specifications previously only found in extremely expensive high-end models to an accessible level, paving the way for the next generation of standards.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have scattered dents and scratches.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume, Pickup Balancer, Treble/Bass, Middle/Frequency, EQ Bypass Switch
Nut Width: 49.4mm
Bridge String Spacing: 18mm (Variable)
Scale Length: 33-35 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 2023
Country of Manufacture: INDONESIA

During setup, we use D'addario EXL strings, highly trusted by professional musicians.

Characterized by a powerful attack and rich harmonics, these strings truly deserve to be called a standard.

These high-quality strings bring out the maximum performance and character of the instrument, so please enjoy them as well.

GIB craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.

Selling my Höfner short-scale bass. It’s a great-playing bass that’s comfortable to wear for long rehearsals or gigs thanks to the short scale, but still has plenty of punch from the dual humbuckers.

The black finish, tortoise pickguard, and block inlays give it a cool vintage vibe that’s a little different than the typical Fender-style bass. It’s got a lot of personality without being over the top.

Overall it’s in good condition with normal signs of use. Everything works as it should, and it’s ready to play.

I’m just thinning the herd and don’t find myself reaching for this one enough to justify keeping it.

item.title: YAMAHA BROAD BASS BB434 Teal Blue [IKY074066] (07/16) — $566.29
item.description:
$566.29 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

The 400 Series is a modern version of the BROAD BASS, a Yamaha original bass that has been in production since 1977.
It features a uniquely rounded alder body, a pickguard that adds visual interest, and PJ pickups.

It employs a miter bolting system that uses four screws to secure the neck vertically from the back of the body, plus two additional screws that secure the neck end at a 45-degree angle from the back of the body.
This firmly bonds the body and neck, producing a powerful tone that feels as though the body and neck are a single unit.
Compared to a standard bolt-on joint, this design minimizes energy loss from string vibration, delivering rich sustain and an overwhelming body resonance.

The bridge is designed for through-body string routing, allowing for individual string tension adjustment.
While there are some minor scratches and dents throughout, it shows little wear and is in good condition.
It has been fully serviced and cleaned at our repair center, and playability is excellent.

A Precision Bass from the "Silver Series" lineup of Japanese-made Squier guitars.

Its serial number is gO003426,h and it is a Japanese-made instrument manufactured around 1993?1994.

The SPB-33 is a distinctive model that retains the classic Precision Bass style while incorporating a wood-fiber material called gWOOD FIBERh into the body.Produced as an ambitious experiment by Fujigen at the time, it is now attracting attention as a rare specification. Featuring a maple neck, rosewood fingerboard, and the split-coil pickups characteristic of the Precision Bass, it delivers thick, focused bass and a powerful midrange. You can enjoy the classic Precision Bass sound that excels across a wide range of genres, from rock to pop.

Overall, there are only a few minor scratches, and no major, noticeable damage. While there are relatively few noticeable scratches, there are signs of paint deterioration, and the instrument shows signs of wear appropriate for its age.
In addition to the neck being high, there is a slight reverse bow toward the 1st string side, along with twisting and waviness. The frets have been leveled, and there are currently no issues with playability.

item.title: 【used】Sago / Ove 5 GIB Custom Order 3.7kg #CA22101001【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $3,519
item.description:
$3,519 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This is a like-new used item that was previously ordered and sold through GIB.

The following is the description I created at the time:

---
Sago is a handmade guitar brand located in Hyogo Prefecture, specializing in full custom-made guitars that fulfill all customer requests.

They also create regular models (although most are one-offs) utilizing the knowledge and skills they have cultivated over many years, and these are highly regarded.

It's a brand that enjoys strong trust from musicians, with Tabuchi-san of Unison Square Garden, RIO-san of Mary's Blood, and Kusakari-san of Sakanaction being among the Sago artists.

This model is the Ove, an original model supported by many artists.

I requested a white and gray wrap finish, and the result is even more elegant and beautiful than I imagined. Truly impressive.

The body is made of alder, the neck is maple, and the fretboard is rosewood.

It's a traditional build.

The bridge is a HIPSHOT Style A, and the tuning keys are HIPSHOT Ultralite.

While a GOTOH bridge would have been a good choice, given its Japanese origin, HIPSHOT was chosen to incorporate Western nuances into both the sound and appearance.

The controls are Volume (switchable to passive), Balancer, Tone, and Treble/Bass.

Basically, sufficient low-end and thickness are obtained even in passive mode, so it sounds good even if you use that as the main setting.

Therefore, the EQ is useful for both adding and subtracting frequencies. (Surprisingly, such cases are rare.)

This is a meticulously crafted custom-ordered bass, highly recommended.

---

Fret remaining is approximately 90%.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have no noticeable scratches.

The electronics are in good condition.

No clear modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Master Tone, Treble/Bass
Nut Width: 45.8mm
Bridge String Spacing: 18mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: Unknown
Country of Manufacture: JAPAN

During setup, we use D'addario EXL strings, highly trusted by professional musicians.

Characterized by a powerful attack and rich harmonics, these strings truly deserve to be called a standard.

These high-quality strings bring out the instrument's performance and character to the fullest, so please enjoy them as well.

GIB craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.

Accessories: Gig Bag

Text: Shunsuke Saga

Inspector: Kawami

Product Photos: Lee

GIB 3-Month Warranty Included

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan)

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

The Neo Classic Jazz Bass from FUJIGEN, a Japanese brand renowned for its excellent playability and solid craftsmanship.
It features a classic configuration combining an ash body with a maple neck and maple fingerboard. Its appeal lies in its clear, well-defined sound and tight bass response.Thanks to its proprietary C.F.S. (Circle Fretting System), it offers excellent intonation and chord cohesion, making it a versatile instrument suitable for a wide range of genres. In addition to simple controls?two volume knobs and one tone knob?the push-pull function on the tone knob allows you to switch between series and parallel wiring. Featuring GOTOH hardware, this high-quality Japanese-made Jazz Bass offers excellent tuning stability and playability.

item.title: 1973 Gibson L-9S Ripper USA - Natural w/ OHSC — $2,100
item.description:
$2,100 · Good · TJR Guitars

This is an early production Gibson Ripper Bass manufactured in 1973. The serial number is 174000. Neck and headstock have a flame maple finish. The varitone switch and electronic components (including choke) have been replaced with a modern high quality equivalent. Everything is wired to stock spec. The tone is 100% what you would expect from a Gibson Ripper without the hassle of old and failing electronics. Pictures of electronics can be sent upon request. Original Gibson hard case with red interior included.





Setup professionally in E standard with 45-100 gauge strings.  Truss rod functions properly and turns smoothly.  Neck is straight with slight relief.  Frets are at 40-50% of life.   Action is textbook low.   Electronics work perfectly.  There is plenty of wear and finish checking throughout. There are no structural issues. Overall player’s condition.


Features:
- Maple Body

- Maple Neck w/ Maple Fretboard

- Set Neck Construction

- 34.5” Scale

- 12” Fretboard Radius

- Gibson Ripper Humbucker Pickups w/ Q-System

- USA Made

- Weighs 8.5lbs

- Original Gibson Hard Case Included




Case Condition: Case is very worn but holding together. One latches is broken, the other four work as they should.



If you have any questions please ask.


Fast and insured shipping.  All items packaged professionally.  Need it shipped somewhere not currently offered?  Send us a message and we will get you a quote.

A U.S.-made L-2000 with a pickguard installed!!

The L-2000?arguably Leo Fenderfs final masterpiece!!
Following the Stingray and the Saber, the L-2000 can be considered the ultimate evolution of this design!!

It features a sunburst finish with an alder body, an ebony fingerboard, and a maple neck.
The combination of its proprietary MFD pickups and the Tri-Sound system allows for a wide range of sound shaping!!
With a uniquely designed bridge featuring a saddle-lock system and medium-jumbo frets, this guitar truly lives up to its reputation by incorporating specifications that still hold up today!!

The previous owner installed a pickguard, but intentionally left space near the front pickup to ensure therefs plenty of room for your thumb. The tuning pegs have been replaced with paddle-style pegs.
There is a dent near the headstock logo, and while there are other minor imperfections, no damage is visible that significantly detracts from the overall appearance.
It has been fully adjusted since arrival; the neck and truss rod are in good condition, and this is a guitar that will serve you well for years to come.
